segments.p1-1-g61: Я is the nominative subject 'I'. уже давно is an adverbial phrase: уже ('already') + давно ('for a long time'), emphasizing a continuing state that began in the past and still holds. так modifies живу and means 'like this' or 'that way'; живу is present tense, 1st person singular, imperfective (literally 'I live'), expressing an ongoing habitual/state rather than a completed action. The dash introduces a duration specification: лет двадцать ('about twenty years'), a colloquial time expression where лет is the genitive plural used with numerals to indicate years.
segments.p1-1-g62: Теперь is an adverb 'now' setting the temporal context. мне is the dative pronoun 'to me' used in the Russian age construction (literally 'to me forty'), so the clause means 'I am forty.' сорок is the numeral 'forty' naming the age. The sentence omits a copula (есть) because Russian commonly uses the dative + numeral construction to give age.
segments.p1-1-g63: Я is the subject 'I'. прежде is an adverb 'formerly/before' indicating a past habitual state. служил is the past tense, masculine singular of служить (imperfective), 'served' or 'held service' — here meaning service in a post or office; it describes a past, now-ended regular occupation.
segments.p1-1-g64: а is a contrasting conjunction 'but/and yet'. теперь is 'now' showing a change from the previous state. не служу is the present-tense 1st person singular verb with negation ('I do not serve / I am not serving'), indicating that the previous service has ceased; служу is imperfective present, here used to state the current (non-)occupation.
segments.p1-1-g65: Я is the subject 'I'. был is the past tense singular masculine of быть ('was'), the copula linking subject and predicate. злой modifies чиновник and here carries the sense 'spiteful/malicious' rather than simply 'angry'; note that in standard modern Russian the predicate after был would often be in the instrumental (e.g. злым чиновником), but Dostoevsky uses a nominative adjective + noun sequence, an older/colloquial pattern that still conveys 'I was a ...'. чиновник is the noun 'official/bureaucrat', the role the speaker held.
segments.p1-1-g66: This is a simple past-tense statement with two coordinated predicates. Я был груб uses the short-form adjective груб (masculine), which functions predicatively after the copula был ('was'); short adjectives are common in predicative position. находил в этом удовольствие uses the imperfective past находил (literally 'found' or 'experienced repeatedly'), plus the prepositional phrase в этом ('in that/this'), to mean 'took pleasure in that.' The conjunction и links the two past actions/habits.
segments.p1-1-g67: Ведь is an emphatic particle meaning 'after all' or 'you see.' The clause has negation with не before the past verb брал ('took/accepted'), expressing that he did not accept bribes. Взяток is in the (genitive) plural form used as the object of брать in this negative construction ('to take bribes').
segments.p1-1-g68: Стало быть is a fixed idiomatic connective meaning 'therefore' or 'so' (literally 'having become, to be'), used to draw a conclusion from the previous clause.
segments.p1-1-g69: Должен был combines the modal adjective/participle должен ('ought to') with the past auxiliary был to express obligation or expectation in the past ('ought to have' / 'had to'). Себя is the reflexive accusative referring back to 'I,' and хоть этим ('at least with this') indicates the minimal compensation; вознаградить is perfective infinitive 'to reward/compensate.' The particle же adds emphasis ('after all/indeed').
segments.p1-1-g70: This is a parenthetical comment in the nominative: плохая (full-form adjective agreeing in gender and number) modifies острота ('witticism/keenness'), literally 'a poor witticism.' The phrase stands alone as an authorial aside.
segments.p1-1-g71: Но introduces a contrast ('but'). Ее is the accusative object pronoun ('it' referring to the previous remark), placed before the verb as is normal in Russian. Вычеркну is the 1st-person singular future of the perfective вычеркнуть ('I will cross out'); with не it means 'I will not cross it out' (here used as an immediate/personal decision).
segments.p1-1-g72: Я ее написал is a past-tense perfective clause: написал is the masculine past of the perfective 'to write' and so reports a completed action ('I wrote it'), with ее as the direct object placed before the verb.
segments.p1-1-g73: Думая is a present active gerund (деепричастие) meaning 'thinking,' linking the manner or reason of the main action to the following subordinate clause; it is attached to the main clause 'I wrote it,' indicating simultaneous mental attitude.
segments.p1-1-g74: Что introduces the content clause ('that it would...'). Выйдет is future tense of the imperfective/semelfactive выйти used here in the sense 'to turn out' or 'come out' (how the writing would be received). Очень остро modifies that verb phrase: очень 'very' + остро 'pointedly/sharply' (adverb), describing the expected tone.
segments.p1-1-g75: А теперь is a transitional phrase 'but now' (а = contrastive 'but/and', теперь = 'now'), marking a change of perspective from the earlier expectation to the present realization.
segments.p1-1-g76: Как увидел сам uses как + past verb to mean 'now that' / 'when' I saw/realized myself. Увидел in this context carries the sense 'noticed/realized,' and сам is an emphatic reflexive 'myself' stressing that the speaker personally saw this.
segments.p1-1-g77: Что хотел только гнусно пофорсить is the content of the realization: что ('that') + хотел (past 'wanted') + только ('only/merely') limits the intention, гнусно ('nasty/disgustingly') modifies пофорсить (colloquial perfective 'to show off'); together: 'that I had only wanted to put on a nasty show.'
segments.p1-1-g78: Нарочно is an adverb meaning 'deliberately/on purpose.' Не вычеркну is the speaker's resolution in the 1st-person future ('I will not cross [it] out'), where the object is implied from earlier "ее." The dash and parenthesis mark this as a defiant afterthought: 'I will deliberately not cross it out.'
segments.p1-1-g25: Непривлекательный is a full adjective (nominative, masculine, singular) modifying человек; the word order (adjective + я + noun) is slightly marked—placing я between adjective and noun gives a self-emphatic, somewhat brusque tone. The clause is a simple nominal sentence with the copula omitted (standard in Russian).
segments.p1-1-g26: Я is the nominative subject 'I'; думаю is first-person singular present of думать and functions as a prosaic reporting verb ('I think'). The comma marks the start of the subordinate clause introduced by что.
segments.p1-1-g27: что introduces the subordinate clause ('that' / here omitted in natural English). у + меня (preposition + genitive) expresses a bodily possession/affliction construction ('I have X' in the sense 'X is hurting me'). болит is 3rd-person singular present of болеть meaning 'aches/harms' with печень as its nominative subject placed at the end: literally 'at me aches the liver' → 'my liver hurts.'
segments.p1-1-g28: Впрочем is a discourse adverb meaning 'however' / 'anyway' used to qualify or soften what follows; here it signals a slight change or qualification of the previous statement.
segments.p1-1-g29: This chunk contains a colloquial, emphatic negative: ни шиша (ни + slang шиша) intensifies the negation to mean 'not a bit' or 'not a damn thing.' не смыслю is first-person present of смыслить ('to understand / to have a clue about'); в моей болезни is a prepositional phrase ('about/in my illness'), and then и не знаю наверно is 'and I don't know, for sure' — не знаю is present 'I don't know' and наверно here qualifies the certainty (ironically 'for sure' / 'certainly' or 'really').
segments.p1-1-g30: что introduces an indirect question ('what'); у меня болит repeats the same bodily-construction as earlier: у + genitive indicates possession/affliction and болит (3rd-person singular) means 'aches/is hurting'—literally 'what at me aches.'
segments.p1-1-g31: Я is the subject; не лечусь is present first-person reflexive verb лечиться ('I don't receive treatment / I don't get treated'). и connects to the past clause: никогда не лечился is the negated past tense ('I never was treated' / 'I never have been treated'), with никогда (never) + не for negation. The pair contrasts present habit and past experience.
segments.p1-1-g32: хотя is the conjunction 'although' introducing a concessive clause. медицину (accusative) and докторов (accusative plural) are direct objects of уважаю, which is 1st-person present of уважать ('I respect'). The conjunction и between objects can be read simply as 'and' (or slightly emphatic 'even'), and the verb at the end gives a concessive admission ('though I do respect...').
segments.p1-1-g33: К тому же is a fixed phrase meaning “besides” or “in addition.” Here к (a preposition) + тому (dative demonstrative) + же (emphatic particle) combine to introduce an additional point; the clause then states the subject (я) and predicate (суеверен) with еще и adding emphasis ('even/also'). 'До крайности' attaches as a degree phrase ('to an extreme').
segments.p1-1-g34: Ну is an interjection meaning “well” or a soft pause/transition; it signals a slight change of tone or a mild conversational hedging before the next clause.
segments.p1-1-g35: Хоть...настолько forms a concessive/limiting phrase meaning 'at least to such an extent' or 'even so much.' Хоть here means 'at least/even' and настолько is an adverb 'to such an extent/so.'
segments.p1-1-g36: Чтоб introduces a subordinate clause of consequence/result ('that' / 'so that') describing the extent just mentioned; уважать is the infinitive 'to respect' and медицину is the direct object in the accusative — together 'enough to respect medicine.'
segments.p1-1-g37: Я достаточно образован is a simple nominal clause: subject (я) + adverb достаточно ('sufficiently/enough') + short-form adjective образован ('educated'). The short-form adjective functions as the predicative 'am educated.'
segments.p1-1-g38: Чтоб here introduces the result or degree clause 'so as not to be ...' (i.e. 'educated enough that I shouldn't be superstitious'); не negates the infinitive быть ('to be'), and суеверным is the adjective in instrumental/short form used predicatively.
segments.p1-1-g39: Но is the adversative conjunction 'but'; the clause simply repeats the subject+predicate (я суеверен) to state the contradiction. The short-form adjective суеверен again functions as the predicate.
segments.p1-1-g40: Нет‑с is a colloquial/archaic negative interjection: Нет ('no') plus an enclitic particle -с that adds emphasis or a brusque tone; it functions as a turn-taking/contradictory 'No—' at the start of the sentence.
segments.p1-1-g41: Я не хочу лечиться is a present-tense declarative: subject (я) + negation (не) + хочу ('I want') + infinitive лечиться ('to undergo treatment'). Со злости is a prepositional phrase meaning 'out of anger/out of spite' (со + noun in genitive), giving the cause or motive.
segments.p1-1-g42: Вот этого is a demonstrative phrase drawing attention to 'this' thing; вот is a discourse particle ('now/well/see') and этого is the demonstrative pronoun in the accusative/genitive form meaning 'this (thing).' Together they mean roughly 'this (matter)' or 'this point.'
segments.p1-1-g43: Наверно is an adverb meaning 'probably' or 'I suppose' and qualifies the following negative statement; it marks the speaker's conjecture about the listener's reaction.
segments.p1-1-g44: Не изволите понимать is an ironic, slightly archaic/perhaps sarcastic construction: изволите (2nd person plural) is the polite/old-fashioned verb 'to deign' and in negative form it means 'you will not deign'; понимать is the infinitive 'to understand.' The whole phrasing implies 'you probably won't bother/deign to understand this.'
segments.p1-1-g14: В этом отрывке is a prepositional locative phrase: в + the demonstrative adjective этом (prepositional singular) agreeing with отрывке; it sets the location/context ('in this excerpt').
segments.p1-1-g15: озаглавленном is the short/qualifying passive past participle (agreeing in case, number, gender) modifying отрывке; the comma marks a parenthetical participial phrase meaning 'titled/entitled "Подполье"'.
segments.p1-1-g16: это лицо is the nominative subject ('this person/individual'); рекомендует is present tense, 3rd person singular of рекомендовать ('recommends' or 'introduces'); самого себя is the intensified reflexive object (самого + себя in accusative) meaning 'himself/the very self', adding emphasis.
segments.p1-1-g17: свой взгляд is a noun phrase where свой is the reflexive possessive that must refer back to the subject (this person's own), and взгляд here means 'view/opinion' (not merely a physical gaze).
segments.p1-1-g18: и как бы wants to parse как бы together as a modal phrase meaning 'as it were / as if' (как + particle бы makes the statement tentative); хочет is present tense 'wants', and выяснить is the infinitive 'to find out/clarify'; те причины is a demonstrative phrase 'those reasons' that is the object of выяснить.
segments.p1-1-g19: по которым is a relative construction with по + dative plural которых/kоторым linking back to 'те причины' ('by/according to which' or 'for which'); оно (neuter pronoun) functions as the subject of the following verbs and refers to 'это лицо' or 'that being'; явилось is past perfective 'appeared/came into being'; и должно было явиться combines the modal должно было (modal in the past: 'was supposed/ought to') + infinitive явиться, so 'and ought to have appeared'; в нашей среде is a locative phrase 'in our milieu/among us'.
segments.p1-1-g20: The whole sentence is packaged as one clause. В следующем отрывке is a locative/time phrase (в + prepositional следую́щем agreeing with отрывке). Придут is plural future/present perfective of прийти ('will come/appear') and agrees with the delayed subject настоящие "записки" (inverted word order: verb before subject). Уже is an adverb 'already'; настоящие modifies записки ('real, genuine notes'); этого лица is genitive 'of this person' (notes belonging to him); о некоторых событиях его жизни is a prepositional phrase 'about certain events of his life' (его = his).
segments.p1-1-g21: This segment contains the author name followed by the opening sentence. Федор Достоевский is simply the author name. Я is the nominative pronoun 'I'; человек is the noun 'person/man' used as the predicate nominal; больной is an adjective functioning as the predicate (Russian omits the copula 'to be' in the present), so 'I am a sick man' or 'I am a man who is sick.'
segments.p1-1-g24: Я is the subject pronoun 'I'; злой is an adjective describing the speaker and here serves as the main quality ascribed to 'человек' (злой человек = a spiteful/evil/angry man); человек is the noun completing the predicate—copula is omitted, so literally 'I [am] a злой person.'
segments.p1-1-g1: The repeated conjunction 'И ... и ...' here functions as a correlative 'both ... and ...', linking two nominative noun phrases that serve together as the sentence subject. 'автор записок' is a noun + genitive modifier ('author of the notes'), and 'самые "Записки"' uses 'самые' as an intensifier meaning 'the very ...' (emphatic 'the ... themselves').
segments.p1-1-g2: 'разумеется' is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'of course' that modifies the whole clause; it's placed between the subject and the predicate for emphasis.
segments.p1-1-g3: 'вымышлены' is the short-form past passive participle (plural) agreeing with the compound subject; it means 'are invented/fictional' and functions as the predicate (copula is omitted in Russian past-tense predicative constructions).
segments.p1-1-g4: 'Тем не менее' is a fixed phrase meaning 'nevertheless'; here it introduces a contrast. 'такие лица' is nominative plural ('such persons'), the head of the clause.
segments.p1-1-g5: 'как' introduces an example or apposition: 'such persons as the author of such notes.' 'сочинитель' is nominative and stands in apposition to 'такие лица'; 'таких записок' is a genitive phrase modifying 'сочинитель' (author of such notes).
segments.p1-1-g6: The correlative construction 'не только ... но даже ...' begins here; 'не только могут' uses the present indicative plural of 'мочь' ('can/may') to state possibility ('not only can').
segments.p1-1-g7: 'но даже' continues the correlative ('but even'), and 'должны' is a predicative adjective/modal in plural meaning 'ought to' or 'should' (more modal than strict obligation). 'существовать' is the infinitive 'to exist', and 'в нашем обществе' is the locative phrase 'in our society'.
segments.p1-1-g8: 'взяв' is a perfective gerund (деепричастие) from 'взять' meaning 'having taken' or more idiomatically here 'taking into account'; it governs the noun phrase 'в соображение те обстоятельства' ('those circumstances into consideration') and forms an adverbial clause explaining the condition for the previous statement.
segments.p1-1-g9: 'при которых' is a relative prepositional phrase ('under/at which') introducing a subordinate clause; 'вообще' means 'in general'; 'складывалось' is past imperfective (neuter singular to agree with 'наше общество') of 'складываться' meaning 'took shape/was formed', so the clause reads 'under which, in general, our society was formed.'
segments.p1-1-g10: 'Я хотел' is past-tense 'I wanted/wished' expressing intention; 'вывести' is the perfective infinitive 'to bring out/present' and 'перед лицо публики' ('before the face of the public') is an idiomatic object phrase meaning 'before the public' (here 'перед' + noun phrase functions to indicate 'in front of / to the attention of').
segments.p1-1-g11: 'повиднее' is a comparative form meaning 'more visible/notable' (comparative of 'видный'); 'обыкновенного' is the comparative standard in genitive ('than the ordinary/usual'), so together they mean 'more conspicuous than usual.'
segments.p1-1-g12: 'один из' = 'one of'; 'характеров' is genitive plural ('of the characters/types'); 'протекшего недавнего времени' is a genitive noun phrase where 'протекшего' is a past participle from 'протекать' used figuratively ('that has passed') modifying 'недавнего времени' ('the recent time' = the recent past).
segments.p1-1-g13: 'Это' is the demonstrative pronoun 'this/it is' functioning as the subject; the dash marks the copular pause. 'один из представителей' means 'one of the representatives' (genitive plural). 'еще' = 'still', and 'доживающего поколения' is a genitive phrase with the present active participle 'доживающего' ('living out, surviving') modifying 'поколения' ('generation'), so the whole phrase means 'one of the representatives of a generation still surviving/coming to its end.'
segments.p1-1-g79: "Когда" introduces a temporal (habitual) clause. "к столу" is the dative phrase 'to/toward the desk' (столу is dative singular), placed before the relative clause to mark the place to which people approached.
segments.p1-1-g80: "у которого" is a relative construction (у + которого) meaning 'at which / by which' (у requires the genitive form of the pronoun). "я сидел" is a simple past tense clause 'I was sitting' describing the narrator's location.
segments.p1-1-g81: Подходили is the past imperfective, plural form meaning 'they approached' or 'would approach' and here expresses repeated/habitual action; the explicit subject is supplied by the following noun phrase ('просители').
segments.p1-1-g82: «бывало» is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'it would happen' / 'as was often the case' and marks that the described approaching was a frequent occurrence.
segments.p1-1-g83: «просители» is the plural noun 'petitioners'; «за справками» is a prepositional phrase meaning 'for certificates / reference papers' with за + instrumental, indicating what they came for.
segments.p1-1-g84: This is the main clause: «я» is the subject; «зубами» is the instrumental plural ('with my teeth'); «на них скрежетал» literally 'I grated/gnashed (my) teeth at them' (скрежетал is imperfective past used for repeated action); «и чувствовал неумолимое наслаждение» joins another predicate: 'and felt an inexorable/relentless pleasure'.
segments.p1-1-g85: This subordinate clause begins with «когда» ('when/whenever') and uses the impersonal past «удавалось» + infinitive to mean 'it happened / I managed' to do something; «кого-нибудь» is an indefinite object 'someone / anybody' and «огорчить» is the perfective infinitive 'to upset'.
segments.p1-1-g86: «Почти всегда» is an adverbial phrase 'almost always' modifying the impersonal past «удавалось», which here means 'I (or one) managed' — a habitual/result clause referring back to the previous infinitive construction.
segments.p1-1-g87: «Большею частию» is an older/poetic instrumental phrase meaning 'for the most part' or 'mostly'. The word order «все был народ робкий» is slightly archaic: it means 'the whole people were timid' (все = everyone/the whole), with был as the past copula and робкий the predicate adjective.
segments.p1-1-g88: «известно» is a parenthetical 'of course / it is known' introducing the explanation 'просители' (petitioners); the dash makes the phrase elliptical: 'of course — petitioners.'
segments.p1-1-g89: «Но» = 'but'. «из фертов» = 'out of / from the ferts' (фертов is an obscure/colloquial group-name in the genitive plural, 'from among the ferts'). «я особенно терпеть не мог» is the set expression 'I especially could not bear/tolerate' (терпеть in the infinitive with не мог in the past). «одного офицера» is the accusative object 'one officer' (animate, marked by the numeral).
segments.p1-1-g90: Он is the nominative pronoun 'he' (subject). Никак не хотел uses никак + negation не with the past-tense хотел to mean 'would not in any way' or 'stubbornly refused' (никак intensifies the negation). Покориться is a reflexive infinitive ('to submit') that completes хотел. И links the two predicates sharing the same subject. Омерзительно is an adverb modifying гремел; гремел is the imperfective past tense 'clattered/clang(ed)' indicating repeated or habitual noise; саблей is the instrumental singular of сабля, showing the instrument used ('with a sabre').
segments.p1-1-g91: У меня is the possessive existential construction 'I had' (literally 'at me'), with у + genitive to indicate possession/existence with быть. С ним is 'with him' (preposition с + instrumental ним) indicating the other party. Полтора года gives the duration 'one and a half years' — полтора governs the genitive года. За эту саблю uses за + accusative here in the sense 'over/for (because of)' to mark the cause or object of dispute. Война is the noun 'war/struggle' (used figuratively here), and была is the past-tense copula 'was', completing the existential 'there was a war (for me)'. The word order places the duration before the noun, a common Russian pattern.
segments.p1-1-g92: Я is the nominative 'I'. Наконец is the adverb 'finally/at last'. Одолел is the past tense (perfective) of одолеть meaning 'to overcome / defeat / get the better of' — the direct object (him) is omitted as understood from the preceding sentence, a common ellipsis in Russian narration.
segments.p1-1-g93: Он is the nominative 'he'. Перестал is the past-tense perfective 'ceased / stopped' and is normally followed by an infinitive to indicate the action that ended; греметь is that infinitive (imperfective) meaning 'to clatter/clang' — here the infinitive names the habitual noise that has ceased. The object 'sabre' is omitted but understood from context.
segments.p1-1-g45: 'Ну-с' is a colloquial/discourse interjection (ну + enclitic -с) meaning roughly 'well then' or 'well'; it introduces the speaker's response and creates a slight pause.
segments.p1-1-g46: А is a contrastive conjunction here ('and/yet/but'), followed by the subject pronoun я (I) and the verb понимаю, present tense, 1st person singular — 'I understand.'
segments.p1-1-g47: Я is the nominative personal pronoun 'I' serving as the sentence subject; the following comma marks a slight pause for the parenthetical remark.
segments.p1-1-g48: Разумеется is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'of course' or 'naturally,' qualifying the speaker's statement that follows.
segments.p1-1-g49: Не сумею is the negated future of суметь ('I will not be able / I won't manage'); вам is the dative 'to you' (indirect object); объяснить is the perfective infinitive 'to explain' and is the complement of сумею.
segments.p1-1-g50: Кому (dative 'to whom') + именно ('exactly') foreground the indirect object of насолю (perfective future 'I will spite / I will do someone a spite'); в этом случае is a locative/time expression 'in this case'; моей злостью is instrumental ('by/with my anger') indicating means.
segments.p1-1-g51: Я знаю is present tense 'I know'; отлично хорошо are stacked adverbial intensifiers ('perfectly / very well') modifying знаю to give strong emphasis.
segments.p1-1-g52: Что introduces a content clause ('that'); и докторам places 'to the doctors' fronted and here carries the sense 'even to the doctors' (и = even/also) for emphasis; я никак не смогу is a negated ability in the future ('I absolutely will not be able'); нагадить is used figuratively ('to make a mess to harm/insult') and тем 'by that / thereby' points back to the means already mentioned.
segments.p1-1-g53: Что у них не лечусь is a subordinate clause 'that I am not treated by them / that I don't take treatment from them'; у них denotes the people/place with whom treatment would occur, and лечусь is the reflexive verb лечиться in the 1st person present negated.
segments.p1-1-g54: Я знаю again 'I know'; лучше всякого is a comparative idiom meaning 'better than anyone' (лучше is comparative 'better' modifying знáю, with всякого in genitive as the standard of comparison).
segments.p1-1-g55: Что всех этим (всем этим) я единственно только себе поврежу expresses the content 'that by all this I shall only damage myself' — всем этим is instrumental ('by all this'), единственно and только double-mark exclusivity ('only / solely'), себе is dative reflexive ('to myself') and поврежу is future 'I will damage'; и никому больше closes the clause with 'and to no one else' or 'and no one else (will be harmed)'.
segments.p1-1-g56: Но is the adversative conjunction 'but'; все-таки is a fused particle meaning 'still / nevertheless / after all' that moderates the statement — together they introduce a concessive turn.
segments.p1-1-g57: Если introduces a conditional clause 'if'; я не лечусь is present-tense negated reflexive 'I don't undergo treatment / I'm not being treated.'
segments.p1-1-g58: Так это = 'then/that is (so)' and со злости uses the preposition со + noun in the instrumental to mean 'out of spite / from anger' (indicating motive).
segments.p1-1-g59: Печенка (colloquial for 'печень') is the nominative subject 'the liver' (spoken tone), and болит is 3rd person singular present of болеть meaning 'aches' or 'hurts.'
segments.p1-1-g60: Так вот пускай же is an emphatic optative construction: так вот 'so/then' + пускай (subjunctive/imperative particle 'let') + же (emphatic). Ее refers back to the liver ('let it') and еще крепче болит is a comparative intensification 'ache even more / more severely' with крепче the comparative of крепко meaning 'more intensely.' The whole is an exclamatory 'let it hurt even more!'
segments.p1-1-g113: Даже is an adverb of emphasis placed before душой to mean 'even'; душой is the instrumental singular used adverbially ('with the soul' = inwardly); умилюсь is 1st person singular future (perfective, reflexive) meaning 'I will be moved/touched' and takes the instrumental to indicate the inner feeling.
segments.p1-1-g114: Хоть is a concessive particle here ('although/even if') introducing the following contrast; уж is a colloquial/emphatic particle (not temporal) strengthening the concession and is set off by the comma.
segments.p1-1-g115: Наверно is an adverb modifying the whole following clause, giving the speaker's estimation ('probably').
segments.p1-1-g116: Потом is an adverb 'later'; буду is the auxiliary (1st person singular) forming the future for the coordinated imperfective verbs that follow (буду + infinitive); вам is dative indicating 'before you/in your presence'; на + себя ('на себя') is a combination where на with the accusative reflexive себя marks the target of the reproach ('at myself'); скрежетать (infinitive) with зубами (instrumental) means 'to gnash/grind with (one's) teeth'; и coordinates the second infinitive страдать, which, together with the single auxiliary буду, yields a parallel future 'will suffer'; от introduces the cause ('from/because of shame'); несколько месяцев gives the duration, and бессонницей is instrumental with страдать meaning 'to suffer from insomnia'.
segments.p1-1-g117: Таков is a short-form demonstrative/adjectival pronoun functioning as the predicate ('such is'); уж is an emphatic particle adding insistence; мой обычай is the nominative subject phrase ('my habit/custom'). The predicate-before-subject order emphasizes the character of the habit.
segments.p1-1-g118: 'Это' at the start is an emphatic/existential particle ('it / this'), making the clause emphatic: 'It was I who lied...'. 'Наврал' is past masculine perfective 'lied', 'про себя' = 'about myself', 'давеча' = 'recently/the other day' (colloquial).
segments.p1-1-g119: The subordinate clause introduced by 'что' reports the content of the lie: 'that I was a злой чиновник.' 'Был' is past tense 'was', and 'злой' describes the noun 'чиновник' (here meaning 'ill-tempered/angry' or 'malicious' official; context favors 'angry/spiteful official').
segments.p1-1-g120: 'Со' is a variant of the preposition 'с' used before certain consonant clusters; with the noun in genitive 'злости' it expresses cause or motive ('out of anger, from spite'). 'Наврал' is past tense 'lied.'
segments.p1-1-g187: До + noun in the genitive (До семидесяти лет) marks a temporal limit meaning “up to/until seventy years.” Семидесяти is the declined numeral ‘seventy’ after до (genitive), and лет is the genitive plural of год used with numerals. Проживу is 1st person singular future (perfective) — the subject 'я' is omitted as normal in Russian — giving an emphatic exclamation about living until that age.
segments.p1-1-g188: Grammar mirrors the previous sentence: До + genitive (До восьмидесяти лет) sets the temporal limit “until/even to eighty.” Восьмидесяти is the numeral in genitive after до; лет is genitive plural. Проживу remains the 1st person singular future (perfective), with the pronoun omitted, forming a forceful exclamation.
segments.p1-1-g191: Постойте is the polite/plural imperative (2nd person plural) of постоять (perfective), used as an interjection meaning “wait” or “hold on.” Its perfective aspect implies a short, momentary action (“stand/wait for a bit”), and the form is direct and exclamatory.
segments.p1-1-g192: Дайте is the imperative plural/polite of дать used here in the sense “allow/let (me).” The indirect object мне is omitted but implied. Дух перевести is an idiomatic infinitival phrase meaning “to catch one’s breath” (literally “to transfer/bring a breath”); перевести is the infinitive used with дать to express permitting an action.
segments.p1-1-g121: Subject Я (I) + adverb просто (simply) sets the manner. The phrase баловством занимался uses the noun баловство in the instrumental with заниматься (занимался) to mean ‘occupied myself with mischief/playing about.’ The repeated и ... и with с + instrumental (с просителями и с офицером) lists the people he amused himself with: “with petitioners and with the officer.”
segments.p1-1-g122: А introduces a contrast (‘but’). В сущности is a fixed phrase meaning ‘in essence’ or ‘in truth.’ Никогда не мог сделаться is past-tense modal + perfective: мог (past of мочь) + делать/сделаться (here reflexive сделаться, ‘to become’) expresses inability in the past — ‘could never become.’ Злым is the predicate adjective (instrumental/adjectival complement after сделаться) meaning ‘spiteful/mean.’
segments.p1-1-g123: Поминутно (minute-by-minute) is an adverb modifying сознавал (imperfective past ‘was aware of’), so the speaker stresses repeated/continuous awareness. В себе (‘within myself’) is the locative/reflexive expression. Много‑премного is a stylistic/archaic intensifier meaning ‘very many; an awful lot.’ The noun phrase самых противоположных тому элементов is in the genitive (много элементов), with противоположных тому (‘opposed to that’) qualifying элементов.
segments.p1-1-g124: 'Я чувствовал' is past imperfective ('I felt / I was aware'), indicating ongoing perception rather than a single momentary event.
segments.p1-1-g125: The subordinate clause 'что они так и кишат во мне' — 'что' introduces it; 'они' is the subject 'they'; 'так и' is an idiomatic combination adding emphasis ('just so / indeed / really'); 'кишат' is present tense plural 'swarm/teem'; 'во мне' ('в' variant 'во' before consonants) means 'in me/inside me.'
segments.p1-1-g126: This final noun phrase is an appositive: 'эти противоположные элементы' clarifies who 'they' are — 'these opposing elements.' It repeats in nominative plural and follows the clause for emphasis.
segments.p1-1-g127: Subject 'Я' (I) with the past-tense masculine verb 'знал' (knew). The past tense here is the imperfective past of знать and simply states the narrator's knowledge; the comma introduces the following subordinate clause.
segments.p1-1-g128: 'что' is the conjunction 'that' introducing the content clause; 'они' is the clause subject 'they'. 'всю жизнь' is a time expression 'all (my) life' placed before the verb for emphasis. 'во' is the euphonic variant of the preposition 'в' used before the pronoun 'мне' (here 'in me'), and 'кишели' is the past plural of 'кишеть' (imperfective) meaning 'teemed/swam' — indicating a repeated or continuous state. The conjunction 'и' links a second verb, with 'из меня' ('out of me', using genitive), the particle 'вон' as an emphatic 'right/out', and 'наружу' as the directional adverb 'outward'; 'просились' (past plural of 'проситься') expresses that they were pressing/trying to get out.
segments.p1-1-g129: 'но' is the adversative conjunction 'but'. The clause 'я их не пускал' uses 'я' (I) plus 'их' as the direct-object pronoun 'them' (accusative), and 'пускал' is the imperfective past of 'пускать' with 'не' negating it — indicating he repeatedly or habitually did not allow them.
segments.p1-1-g130: A bare repetition 'не пускал' (didn't let) omitting subject and object for rhetorical emphasis; grammatically it's the same negative imperfective past verb phrase as before, continuing the refusal.
segments.p1-1-g131: 'нарочно' is an adverb meaning 'deliberately' or 'on purpose' placed before the verb for emphasis. 'не пускал наружу' again uses the negative imperfective past 'did not let' plus the directional adverb 'наружу' ('out'/'to the outside'), completing the thought: he purposely would not let them out.
segments.p1-1-g178: Я вам скажу is a first-person future statement: сказа́ть (perfective) in the 1st person singular (скажу) expresses 'I will tell'. Вам is the dative 'to you' (indirect object); the order Я (subject) + вам (dative) + скажу (verb) is normal and gives a direct 'I'll tell you'.
segments.p1-1-g179: кто живет is a nominal/relative clause 'who lives' introducing the content; живёт is present tense (indicative) and here means 'leads a life / actually lives' rather than merely 'resides'. The colon that follows signals that an explanation or specification will follow.
segments.p1-1-g180: дураки и негодяи живут is a simple declarative clause with a plural subject (дураки и негодяи) and present-tense verb живут. The repetition of 'live' (жить) expresses a general, almost proverbial truth: 'It is fools and scoundrels who live.'
segments.p1-1-g181: Я всем старцам это в глаза скажу: всем старцам is dative plural 'to all the elders'; это is the direct object 'this'; в глаза is an adverbial idiom 'to the (very) face' or literally 'into the eyes' and adds force. Скажу is future 1st person singular 'I will say/tell'.
segments.p1-1-g182: всем этим почтенным старцам is a repeated dative phrase for emphasis: всем этим 'to all these' (demonstrative + quantifier) + почтенным (adjective 'respected' in dative plural) + старцам (dative plural 'elders'). The adjectives agree in case, number, and gender with старцам.
segments.p1-1-g183: всем этим сребровласым и благоухающим старцам again repeats and accumulates epithets: сребровласым 'silver‑haired' and благоухающим 'fragrant' are adjectival/participial forms in dative plural modifying старцам. The rhythm of repetition heightens the ironic or biting tone.
segments.p1-1-g184: Всему свету в глаза скажу is emphatic: все́му свету (dative) 'to the whole world' combined with в глаза 'to their faces/into the eyes' and the future verb скажу 'I will tell'. The construction stacks datives/adverbials for rhetorical force: 'I'll tell it to the whole world to their faces.'
segments.p1-1-g185: Я имею право так говорить is a set expression: имею (present 'have') + право (noun 'the right') + infinitive говорить 'to speak/say' yields 'I have the right to speak thus/like that'. Так modifies how he speaks ('in that manner').
segments.p1-1-g186: потому что сам до шестидесяти лет доживу is a causal clause introduced by потому что 'because'. Сам is an emphatic reflexive meaning 'I myself' (stressing the subject). До шестидесяти лет uses до + genitive to mean 'until/up to sixty years (of age)', and доживу is perfective future 'I will live/survive until' (дожить).
segments.p1-1-g168: Это here is a demonstrative pronoun functioning like the copula "this is." Сорокалетнее is the neuter nominative singular form of the adjective сорокалетний, agreeing with the neuter noun убеждение and meaning "forty‑year (long)" — i.e. a conviction that has lasted forty years. Мое is a possessive pronoun modifying убеждение; the word order (adjective before possessive) is slightly emphatic but grammatically standard.
segments.p1-1-g169: Мне is the dative form of the first‑person pronoun used in Russian to express age (literally "to me (there is) now forty years"). Теперь is the adverb "now." Сорок лет is a numeral + noun phrase: сорок governs the noun год in the genitive plural (лет) to express "forty years (of age)."
segments.p1-1-g170: А is a contrastive conjunction here ("and/yet/and yet"). Ведь is a particle meaning "after all" that adds emphasis. Сорок лет is the subject phrase. The dash plus это forms an equational structure ("— this/that is"). Вся modifies жизнь (feminine) and means "whole/entire," so вся жизнь = "an entire life."
segments.p1-1-g171: Ведь again is the emphasizing particle "after all." Это is the demonstrative pronoun/predicative "this/that is." Самая is the superlative marker "the very/most" and agrees with глубока́я (feminine). Глубокая here is used figuratively — "profound" rather than merely physical depth — and старость means "old age;" together: "the very depth of old age."
segments.p1-1-g172: Дальше is an adverb meaning "beyond/after" (here "beyond forty"). Сорока лет again expresses the age in genitive plural. Жить is the infinitive "to live" used as a verbal noun/predicate ("living [past forty]"). Неприлично is a predicative adverb meaning "indecent/improper," giving the speaker's moral judgment.
segments.p1-1-g173: Пошло is the short‑form (neuter) predicative of пошлый, meaning "vulgar," "banal," or "tasteless." As a short adjective it agrees in gender (neuter) with the implicit neuter nominal clause ("to live [so] is vulgar"). Note: the supplied lemma/senses in the data appear to be for the verb пойти (to go), but here the word is the adjective пошлый in short form.
segments.p1-1-g174: Безнравственно is an adverb/predicative meaning "immoral" or "depraved"; used here as a final exclamatory judgment echoing неприлично and пошло.
segments.p1-1-g175: Кто is the interrogative pronoun "who." Живет is the present tense, 3rd‑person singular of жить, here forming the main verb of the question. Дольше is the comparative adverb "longer/beyond" used with a time phrase; сорока лет is the numeral + noun in genitive plural indicating the time measure ("forty years"). Together: "Who lives longer than forty (years)?"
segments.p1-1-g176: Отвечайте is the imperative (plural or polite singular) form of отвечать: "answer." Искренно is an adverb "sincerely," modifying the imperative and requesting a candid reply.
segments.p1-1-g177: Честно is an adverb meaning "honestly" used alone as a question tag or intensifier — "honestly?" — asking for truthful, fair responding.
segments.p1-1-g94: Впрочем is an introductory adverb/particle here, functioning like English 'however' or 'besides' to qualify what follows; it is parenthetical and does not affect the sentence's core grammar.
segments.p1-1-g95: Это случилось is a neuter-past construction: случилось is the perfective past (neuter singular) agreeing with the demonstrative это as the grammatical subject; ещё в моей молодости is a time expression ('still/in my youth') with в + prepositional (моей молодости).
segments.p1-1-g96: Знаете ли is a direct question form: знаете is 2nd-person-plural present of знать ('do you know'), and the enclitic ли turns the clause into a yes/no/introductory question (here functioning like 'do you know...'). Но is a contrastive conjunction 'but'.
segments.p1-1-g97: Господа is a vocative-like address (plural) meaning 'gentlemen' or 'sirs'; it stands outside the clause as an interjectional addressee.
segments.p1-1-g98: В чем состоял... is an interrogative nominal clause: в чем = 'in what', состоял is past tense (masculine singular) of состоят/состоять 'consist (in)' and agrees implicitly with пункт; главный пункт моей злости is a noun phrase where моей злости is a genitive attribute 'of my anger'.
segments.p1-1-g99: Да в том-то и состояла вся штука is an emphatic formula. Да is an interjection/affirmation; в том-то (the demonstrative том plus the emphatic particle -то) stresses 'in that very thing'; и связывает the emphasis with состояла ('consisted/was'); вся штука is colloquial 'the whole thing' (штука used as 'matter/point').
segments.p1-1-g100: В том-то и заключалась наибольшая гадость repeats the demonstrative + emphatic -то and uses заключаться (imperfective past) meaning 'to lie in / to consist in' followed by the noun phrase наибольшая гадость 'the greatest nastiness' (гадость = vileness/disgust).
segments.p1-1-g101: Что introduces a content clause ('that ...'); я is the subject 'I'; поминутно is an adverb meaning 'minute by minute', marking repeated/continuous occurrence and linking to the imperfective verbs that follow.
segments.p1-1-g102: Даже в минуту самой сильнейшей желчи is a parenthetical time/condition: даже 'even'; в минуту takes the accusative to mean 'at the moment (of)'; самой сильнейшей is a superlative attributive ('the very strongest'); желчи (here figurative) is used as 'bile' meaning 'bitterness/venom' (i.e. extreme spite).
segments.p1-1-g103: Постыдно сознавал в себе combines an adverb 'shamefully' (постыдно) with сознавал, imperfective past of сознавать, here meaning 'I was ashamed to admit / I acknowledged' (imperfective indicates repeated/ongoing recognition), and в себе is a dative/locative reflexive phrase 'in myself' ('to myself').
segments.p1-1-g104: Что я не только не злой is the first contrast clause introduced by что: не только ... but sets up the 'not only' construction; the double не только не is a Russian pattern meaning 'not only not X', here stressing the surprising lack of anger (злой).
segments.p1-1-g105: Но даже и не озлобленный человек continues the contrast: но 'but'; even 'даже и' adds emphasis; не + participle озлобленный (past passive participle used adjectivally) means 'not embittered'; человек is the noun 'person/man' qualifying the speaker.
segments.p1-1-g106: Что я только воробьев пугаю напрасно и себя этим тешу is a second explanatory clause: что 'that'; только here = 'merely/only'; воробьев is genitive plural 'of sparrows' (object of пугаю in the sense 'I frighten sparrows'); пугаю is present imperfective 'I frighten'; напрасно 'in vain/needlessly'; и links to себя этим тешу 'and I console/amuse myself with it' (себя reflexive accusative, этим 'with this/with that', тешу present imperfective 'I comfort/amuse myself').
segments.p1-1-g107: "У меня пена у рта" is a colloquial possession + locative phrasing: 'У меня' (genitive after у) marks that I possess something, here 'пена' (froth), and the second 'у рта' uses у + genitive to indicate location 'at the mouth.' The verb 'есть' is omitted (typical in spoken/colloquial Russian).
segments.p1-1-g108: The particle 'а' here links/contrasts the next imperative request to the previous clause ('and/but now...'). 'Принесите' is the polite plural/imparative perfective 'bring (me)'. 'Какую-нибудь' is an indefinite phrase ('some kind of'); 'какую' is the interrogative/relative base combined with 'нибудь' to make it indefinite.
segments.p1-1-g109: 'Дайте' is the imperative 'give (me)'. 'Чайку' here is the colloquial diminutive meaning 'a little tea / a cup of tea' (but the provided lemma/senses list does not reflect that), 'с' marks accompaniment 'with', and 'сахарцем' is the instrumental/diminutive 'with a lump of sugar.'
segments.p1-1-g110: Simple nominative pronoun 'я' = 'I', placed here as the subject for the following verb.
segments.p1-1-g111: 'Пожалуй' is a modal particle meaning 'perhaps / I suppose' and softens the assertion.
segments.p1-1-g112: И here functions as an intensifying/particle sense ('even' / 'indeed') before the future reflexive verb 'успокоюсь' (1st person singular, perfective: 'I will calm down').
segments.p1-1-g132: Они is the plural personal pronoun 'they' (subject). мучили is the past tense, 3rd person plural imperfective of мучить, meaning 'to torment' or 'to torture' (an ongoing or repeated past action). меня is the accusative object 'me'. до + genitive (до стыда) expresses extent: 'up to' or 'to the point of' (here idiomatically 'to the point of shame').
segments.p1-1-g133: до конвульсий ('до' + genitive) marks the extent 'to the point of convulsions'. меня (acc.) is again the object. доводили is past tense, 3rd person plural of доводить, used here in the sense 'to drive/bring (someone) into a state'. и simply links clauses 'and'. надоели is past plural of надоесть meaning 'became tiresome/annoying', used with the dative мне ('to me'); наконец is an adverb 'finally/in the end'. The dash highlights the break or exclamation.
segments.p1-1-g134: как here is an exclamatory intensifier equivalent to 'how!' or 'what (a)…!' and emphasizes the following verb. надоели is repeated in past plural and refers back to 'they' (implied subject), making the exclamation stronger: 'How they annoyed me!'
segments.p1-1-g135: Уж is a particle giving emphasis (here 'already' or 'indeed'); the phrase не ... ли with кажется forms the interrogative 'doesn't it seem...?' (ли is the enclitic interrogative particle). кажется is 3rd person singular of казаться and takes a dative experiencer (вам), so кажется ли вам = 'does it not seem to you?'.
segments.p1-1-g136: господа is the plural form used vocatively to address the audience — 'gentlemen' (an address, not a grammatical subject here).
segments.p1-1-g137: что introduces the subordinate content clause 'that'. я is the subject 'I'. теперь 'now' sets the time. в чем-то (в + interrogative pronoun + -то) is an indefinite phrase meaning 'in something' or 'in some respect' ('in some way'). перед вами ('перед' + instrumental) means 'in front of you' / 'before you' (i.e. in your presence). раскаиваюсь is 1st singular present of раскаиваться (to repent/be sorry), here 'I am repentant/express remorse'.
segments.p1-1-g138: что again introduces the second subordinate clause. я 'I'. в чем-то 'in some way' (same indefinite meaning). у вас with просить ('просить у кого') indicates the source/indirect object 'from you' (i.e. 'ask you for something'). прощенья is a colloquial/contracted form of прощение ('forgiveness') functioning as the direct object in the genitive/accusative sense used with просить; прошу is 1st person singular present 'I ask/ beg'. Word order places the indefinite and indirect object before the verb for emphasis.
segments.p1-1-g141: Я is the subject 'I'. уверен is the short-form adjective 'sure'/'certain' used predicatively (no copula required in present tense): 'I am sure'.
segments.p1-1-g142: что introduces the content clause. вам is the dative experiencer 'to you'. это 'this/it' is the notional subject of кажется. кажется is 3rd person singular present of казаться meaning 'it seems' or 'it appears' (here 'it seems to you').
segments.p1-1-g145: А is a contrasting conjunction 'but/and' and впрочем is an adverb 'however/in any case', together softening or changing the speaker's tone: 'But, in any case...'.
segments.p1-1-g146: уверяю is 1st singular present of уверять 'to assure/convince' and вас is the accusative object 'you': 'I assure you'. This is a direct speech formula addressing the audience.
segments.p1-1-g147: что introduces the clause. мне is the dative experiencer 'to me'. все равно is an idiomatic phrase meaning 'all the same' / 'it doesn't matter' / 'I don't care' (literally 'all equal'); here it expresses indifference: 'it's all the same to me'.
segments.p1-1-g148: если is 'if'. и here functions concessively ('even'), so если и кажется = 'even if it seems so'. кажется is 3rd person singular 'it seems/appears' used impersonally (no explicit dative here), meaning 'it appears (that way)'.
segments.p1-1-g240: Ну так is a colloquial discourse marker meaning “well then” or “very well.” И я places the particle и before the pronoun to mean “I too / I also,” adding emphasis that the speaker will join others in the action. Буду говорить is the imperfective future formed with the auxiliary буду + infinitive говорить, so it expresses an ongoing or habitual future action (“will be speaking / will speak” rather than a single completed act), and о себе is о + the reflexive pronoun in the prepositional case meaning “about myself.”
segments.p1-1-g151: This is an elliptic clause: the predicate verb (e.g. 'быть' or 'сделаться') is omitted, so 'злым' (instrumental) functions as a predicate adjective referring back to the subject 'Я'. The construction 'не только' introduces a contrast ('not only ...') that is completed by the following 'но...'.
segments.p1-1-g152: 'но' introduces contrast ('but'); 'даже и' is an emphatic combination meaning 'even'; 'ничем' is instrumental and, together with the negative 'не', forms 'ничем не сумел сделаться' = 'couldn't become anything' (instrumental marks what one becomes). 'сумел' is past tense (masculine singular) of 'суметь' meaning 'managed/succeeded', and it takes the infinitive 'сделаться' (to become).
segments.p1-1-g153: 'ни ...' begins the correlative negative list (neither/nor); 'злым' is again instrumental, continuing the series of predicates that he failed to become. The shortness of the phrase relies on the previous verb 'сумел сделаться'.
segments.p1-1-g154: Same correlative 'ни' + adjective in the instrumental case: another item in the enumerated list of things he did not manage to become. Instrumental is the standard case for predicate complements after 'сделаться'.
segments.p1-1-g155: Final item in the 'ни ... ни ...' list: 'подлецом' is instrumental singular of 'подлец' ('scoundrel'), completing the enumeration of identities he could not become.
segments.p1-1-g156: Continuation of the negative enumeration with 'ни' + adjective in instrumental case; 'честным' is a predicate adjective (instrumental) continuing the omitted 'сумел сделаться'.
segments.p1-1-g157: Another item in the 'ни ... ни ...' list; 'героем' (instrumental) functions as a predicate noun—'nor a hero'—again elliptical with reference to the preceding verb 'сделаться'.
segments.p1-1-g158: Final item of the enumeration: 'насекомым' (instrumental) is literal 'an insect' but here used rhetorically for an insignificant creature; continues the same elliptical pattern (omitted verb from earlier clause).
segments.p1-1-g159: 'Теперь' is the temporal adverb 'now'; 'же' is an emphatic/contrastive particle often reinforcing 'now' here. 'Доживаю' is first person singular present of 'доживать' meaning 'to live out (one's life)' and describes an ongoing state. 'В своем углу' is a locative prepositional phrase ('in my corner') with the reflexive possessive 'своем' agreeing with the subject.
segments.p1-1-g160: 'Дразня' is a деепричастие (gerund/participle) meaning 'teasing' and modifies the main verb 'доживаю' (describes how he lives). The phrase 'злобным и ни к чему не служащим утешением' is the instrumental object of 'дразня' — 'with a spiteful and to-no-purpose (i.e. useless) consolation.' 'Ни к чему не служащим' is a participial construction where 'ни к чему' (to nothing) + 'не служащим' (not serving) yields 'useless.'
segments.p1-1-g161: 'Что' introduces a content clause ('that'); 'умный человек' is the subject of that clause. The particle 'и' before the negation ('и не может') adds emphasis: 'cannot even.' 'Может' is present indicative modal 'can'; 'серьезно' is an adverb modifying the verb; 'чем-нибудь' (instrumental + particle) means 'into anything' (indefinite), and is the complement of 'сделаться' (to become).
segments.p1-1-g162: The conjunction 'а' signals contrast ('whereas'/'but'); 'делается' is present reflexive/passive-like 'becomes' (3rd person singular). 'Чем-нибудь' again is instrumental indefinite 'into something', and 'только' ('only') limits the following noun 'дурак' (subject). The word order places the verb 'делается' before the subject 'дурак' for emphasis: 'only a fool becomes anything.'
segments.p1-1-g163: 'Да-с' is an ironic or sardonic interjection (colloquial/archaic). It combines 'да' ('yes') with a particle '-с' and functions as an emphatic 'well, yes' or 'ah yes' — not a literal preposition 'с'.
segments.p1-1-g164: 'Умный человек девятнадцатого столетия' is a noun phrase with 'девятнадцатого столетия' in the genitive as an attributive ('of the nineteenth century'). 'Должен' and 'обязан' are two adjectival modals ('ought to' / 'is obliged') joined by 'и' for reinforcement, both taking the infinitive 'быть'. 'По преимуществу' is an adverbial phrase meaning 'for the most part' and 'бесхарактерным' (instrumental) is the predicate complement: 'to be, for the most part, characterless.'
segments.p1-1-g165: 'Человек же' uses 'же' as a contrastive particle ('on the other hand'); 'с характером' is an instrumental phrase meaning 'with character' (possessive/instrumental indicating attribute). The phrase sets up the opposing type of person to the preceding 'characterless' figure.
segments.p1-1-g166: Single-word apposition: 'деятель' ('man of action'/'doer') is an appositive noun inserted to describe that person; it stands by itself, highlighted by commas, and restates the kind of person being discussed.
segments.p1-1-g167: Dash + predicate phrase: 'существом по преимуществу ограниченным' uses 'существом' and the adjective 'ограниченным' (instrumental) as a predicate (the copula 'is' is omitted). 'По преимуществу' again means 'for the most part', so the clause asserts that such a person is predominantly a limited being. The dash marks the resumptive pause and emphasis.
segments.p1-1-g195: 'Наверно' (colloquial short form of 'наверное') is a sentence-initial modal adverb meaning 'probably' or 'perhaps', used here as a discourse marker to soften or hedge the following assertion.
segments.p1-1-g196: 'вы' is the 2nd-person plural/formal subject; 'думаете' is present indicative 2nd-person plural of 'думать' ('you think/consider'). Together they form a parenthetical main clause introducing the content clause that follows.
segments.p1-1-g197: 'господа' is a vocative/address (plural) — 'gentlemen' — set off by commas to directly address the audience; it does not change the clause structure.
segments.p1-1-g198: 'что' introduces the subordinate content clause ('that ...'); 'я' is the subject, 'вас' the accusative object 'you', 'смешить' is the infinitive 'to make (someone) laugh' and 'хочу' is present 1st-person singular 'I want'. The infinitive precedes the finite verb ('смешить хочу'), a colloquial word order that emphasizes the action (trying to make you laugh) and ends as a rhetorical question.
segments.p1-1-g199: 'Ошиблись' is the past tense (perfective) plural form of 'ошибиться' meaning 'were mistaken' or 'made a mistake'; the subject is omitted but implied (you). 'и' here means 'also/even', and 'в этом' is the prepositional phrase 'about this' or 'in this matter'. The whole sentence is a short, emphatic rebuttal.
segments.p1-1-g200: 'Я' is the subject 'I'; 'вовсе не' is a strong negation meaning 'not at all'; 'такой развеселый человек' is a predicative phrase 'such a cheerful person' (nominative). The clause states a categorical denial of the characteristic.
segments.p1-1-g201: 'как вам кажется' is a standard construction meaning 'as it seems to you' (literally 'how it seems to you'): 'как' = 'as/like', 'вам' = dative 'to you', 'кажется' = impersonal/3rd singular 'it seems'. The repeated 'или как вам' omits 'кажется' and is a colloquial echo (elliptical repetition) adding emphasis.
segments.p1-1-g202: 'может быть' is an idiomatic modal phrase ('perhaps/maybe') formed from 'может' (3rd-person of 'mочь', here indicating possibility) + infinitive 'быть'; it functions as a parenthetical hedge.
segments.p1-1-g203: 'кажется' again is the impersonal/present 'it seems' or 'appears', closing the parenthetical 'может быть, кажется' ('perhaps, it seems'). The semicolon links this to the following qualification.
segments.p1-1-g204: 'впрочем' is a discourse connector meaning 'however' or 'moreover/anyway', introducing a qualification or change of tack.
segments.p1-1-g205: 'если' is the conditional conjunction 'if'; 'вы' is the subject 'you' (plural/formal). This begins the conditional clause that follows.
segments.p1-1-g206: 'раздраженные' is a past-participle/adjectival form 'irritated', agreeing in number with 'вы' and modifying it; 'всей этой болтовней' is an instrumental phrase 'by all this chatter' (instrumental case marking cause/means). The parenthesis then begins with 'а я уже чувствую' ('and I already feel/I can already tell'), where 'чувствую' is 1st-person present 'I feel'.
segments.p1-1-g207: 'что' introduces the subordinate clause 'that you are irritated'; 'вы' is the subject and 'раздражены' is a short-form passive/adjectival predicate meaning 'are irritated' (short adjective used predicatively). The closing parenthesis ends the aside.
segments.p1-1-g208: 'вздумаете' is future 2nd-person plural of 'вздумать' meaning roughly 'should you think/decide' or 'if you happen to decide'; followed by the infinitive 'спросить' ('to ask') and the object 'меня' ('me'). The verbal construction expresses a hypothetical sudden decision to ask.
segments.p1-1-g209: 'кто' is the interrogative 'who'; the particle 'ж' (emphatic) adds force ('who _indeed_'), 'я' is the subject 'I', 'таков' is a predicative adjective meaning 'such' or 'of that sort' (archaic/formal in tone), and 'именно' 'exactly' narrows the question to the precise identity. Word order and particles make this an emphatic rhetorical question.
segments.p1-1-g210: The particle 'то' is the correlative of the conditional ('if ... то ...') and here functions as 'then'; 'я' is the subject 'I'; 'вам' is the dative 'to you', and 'отвечу' is the future 1st-person singular 'I will answer'. Together: 'then I will answer you'.
segments.p1-1-g211: 'я' is the subject 'I'; 'коллежский асессор' is a fixed nominal phrase naming a civil rank ('collegiate assessor'); the absence of a copula in Russian present/future makes this a nominal predicate, and the insertion of 'один' in the original (not listed among the segment's word entries) would convey 'only/merely' — the whole line is a laconic self-identification.
segments.p1-1-g225: Мне говорят is an impersonal reported-speech construction: мне is the dative 'to me' marking the experiencer, and говорят is 3rd-person plural present 'they say' (the agent is unspecified). Together it means 'I'm told' or 'they tell me.'
segments.p1-1-g226: This is a complex subordinate clause introduced by the conjunction что ('that'). климат петербургский is the nominative noun phrase 'the Petersburg climate' (adjective after noun is just word order choice). мне становится вреден uses dative мне ('to me' — the person affected) plus становиться ('becomes') with the short-form adjective вреден as the predicative: 'becomes harmful to me.' The second что introduces a coordinated subordinate clause: с моими ничтожными средствами is an instrumental/with-phrase meaning 'with my meager resources,' and очень дорого ... жить is an impersonal construction where the infinitive жить ('to live') is evaluated by дорого ('is expensive'), so 'it is very expensive to live in Petersburg.'
segments.p1-1-g227: Я все это знаю is a straightforward SVO clause: Я (I) is the subject, все это ('all this') is the direct object, and знаю is 1st-person singular present 'I know' or 'I am aware.'
segments.p1-1-g228: Лучше here is a comparative adverb 'better' modifying the verb знать; the phrase лучше всех этих ... means 'better than all those ...' with всех этих опытных и премудрых советчиков и покивателей in the genitive plural as the standard of comparison. The second зна́ю at the end repeats/ emphasizes the verb ('I know ... better, I know'), a common rhetorical device.
segments.p1-1-g229: Но is 'but' (contrastive). Я остаюсь is present-tense 'I remain / I am staying,' with остаюсь as the 1st-person singular of оставаться. В Петербурге is the locative/ prepositional phrase 'in Petersburg' indicating location.
segments.p1-1-g230: я не выеду uses the perfective verb выехать in the future tense: не выеду = 'I will not leave' (emphatic refusal or decision). из Петербурга is the preposition из plus genitive 'from Petersburg.'
segments.p1-1-g231: Я (I) is the subject; потому is an adverb meaning 'for that reason' or 'therefore,' pointing back to the preceding statement; не выеду is future negative 'I will not leave' (perfective verb showing a resolved action). The whole sentence states a decision motivated by what was just said.
segments.p1-1-g234: A single interjection expressing a sigh or exclamation; no grammatical role beyond emotional exclamation. It stands alone and corresponds to English "ah" or "oh."
segments.p1-1-g235: A colloquial, loosely structured clause: 'да ведь' are discourse particles ("да" here functioning as a conversational 'well/but', reinforced by 'ведь' = 'after all'); 'это совершенно все равно' is a predicative phrase meaning 'this is completely the same / it makes no difference' (literally 'this completely all equal'); the dash introduces a parenthetical specification 'выеду я иль не выеду' — two balanced future verbs linked by 'иль' ('or'), with the pronoun 'я' emphasizing the subject and 'не' negating the second verb. 'Выеду' is the perfective future 1st person singular (single-form future) meaning 'I will leave/go out.'
segments.p1-1-g236: 'А впрочем' is a compact adversative phrase: 'А' as a connective/adversative 'but/and', and 'впрочем' adds 'however/then again' or 'in any case'; the colon signals that a question or clarification follows.
segments.p1-1-g237: 'О чем' = 'about what' (preposition 'о' + interrogative); 'может' is 3rd person singular present of 'мочь' expressing ability/possibility ('can'); 'говорить' is the infinitive 'to speak/talk'; 'порядочный человек' is the noun phrase 'a decent person'; 'с наибольшим удовольствием' is an instrumental-phrase with the superlative adjective 'наибольшим' (most/greatest) modifying 'удовольствием' (with pleasure), indicating manner — together: 'about what can a decent person most enjoy speaking?'. Word order places the modal + infinitive after the question phrase, forming a standard Russian interrogative.
segments.p1-1-g238: A noun 'Ответ' ('Answer') used as a label or heading, followed by a colon to present the reply.
segments.p1-1-g239: 'О себе' is the prepositional phrase 'about oneself' (preposition 'о' + reflexive pronoun 'себе'); it names the topic of the answer and is generic here ('about oneself' or 'about oneself in general').
segments.p1-1-g212: Subject pronoun + past-tense verb: 'Я' is emphasized by preceding the verb. 'служил' is masculine singular past of служить and here means 'to serve' in the sense of holding a (civil) post — not merely to perform a service.
segments.p1-1-g213: 'чтоб' (colloquial contraction of чтобы) introduces a purpose clause: 'in order that'. The clause is impersonal: 'было' is past tense of быть used existentially ('there would be/there was'), followed by 'что-нибудь' ('something', indefinite formed from что + нибудь) and the infinitive/lexical 'есть' here functioning as an existential/predicative ("to be/available") so the whole means 'so that there would be something to eat/available'. The parenthesis 'но единственно для этого' is a restricting aside: 'но' = 'but', 'единственно' = 'only/solely', 'для этого' = 'for that purpose'.
segments.p1-1-g214: 'и когда' links the next clause temporally: 'and when'. 'прошлого года' is a genitive time expression 'last year' ('прошлого' modifies 'года'). 'один из отдаленных моих родственников' literally 'one of my distant relatives' (один из + genitive plural). 'оставил мне' is past tense of оставить with indirect object 'мне' — here 'to bequeath/leave (to me)'. 'шесть тысяч рублей' is the amount (numeral + noun in genitive), and 'по духовному завещанию' means 'according to/by (the) spiritual will/bequest' with по + dative.
segments.p1-1-g215: Main clause with two coordinated past actions: 'я тотчас же вышел в отставку' — 'I immediately left/resigned my post' (вышел в отставку is an idiom: 'to resign'). 'тотчас же' is a fixed phrase meaning 'at once' (же is emphatic here). 'и поселился у себя в углу' — 'and settled down (поселился) at/for myself (у себя) in a corner/nook' — 'у себя' emphasizes it was his own corner.
segments.p1-1-g216: Simple past statement with temporal adverb: 'Я' + 'и прежде жил' — 'I even/also previously lived' or 'I had lived here before'; the particle 'и' here intensifies ('even/also'). 'в этом углу' is a locative/genitive expression 'in this corner'.
segments.p1-1-g217: 'но' introduces contrast: 'but'. 'теперь' = 'now' and the clause 'я поселился в этом углу' repeats the idea with поселился (perfective) meaning he has now taken up residence; word order is straightforward subject–verb–locative.
segments.p1-1-g218: 'Комната моя дрянная' is a nominal sentence with predicative adjective: 'My room (is) lousy.' Russian often omits the copula in present-tense descriptions; 'дрянная' is a strong pejorative adjective meaning 'lousy/rotten'.
segments.p1-1-g219: Single adjective 'скверная' repeats/strengthens the negative description: 'nasty/bad'. It's a coordinate adjective adding flavor to дрянная.
segments.p1-1-g220: 'на краю города' is a prepositional phrase meaning 'on the edge/outskirts of the city' — 'на' + locative phrase 'краю города' (край in dative/locative shape) indicates location at the border/edge of the town.
segments.p1-1-g221: 'Служанка моя - деревенская баба' is a nominative apposition: 'my maid — a country/peasant woman.' The dash marks an appositive shift, and 'баба' here is a coarse term often meaning 'old woman' or peasant woman.
segments.p1-1-g222: Single adjective 'старая' (old) continues the list of negative attributes; adjective after the noun is normal and describes age/state.
segments.p1-1-g223: 'злая от глупости' is a causal description: 'злая' (mean/ill-tempered) followed by 'от' + noun in genitive to indicate cause — 'because of stupidity' (от глупости). The whole phrase means she is nasty as a result of her stupidity.
segments.p1-1-g224: 'и от нее к тому же всегда скверно пахнет' — 'and from her, moreover, it always smells bad.' 'от нее' indicates source ('from her' / 'because of her'); 'к тому же' is a fixed phrase meaning 'besides/on top of that'; 'скверно пахнет' = 'smells badly' (пахнет + adv.).

sentences.p1-1-s1: Both the author of the notes and the "Notes" themselves are, of course, fictional.

sentences.p1-1-s4: This is one of the representatives of a generation that is still living out its days.
